lessons.title: 1. Introduction to C++
lessons.content:
### 🚧 Content Under Maintenance

This lesson is being updated soon!
lessons.backupContent:
> **C++ Mastery:** C++ is a superset of C (mostly).

---

## 📖 Theoretical Foundation

C++ (1985) by Bjarne Stroustrup extends C with Object-Oriented features. It strikes a balance between high-level abstractions and low-level memory manipulation, making it ideal for systems programming, game engines, and high-performance applications.

### ⚙️ Mechanics
- **Efficiency**: C++ gives you direct control over hardware.
- **Flexibility**: Use high-level abstractions or low-level pointers.
- **Safety**: Modern C++ (11/14/17/20) provides safer idioms.

---

## 💻 Code Example

```cpp
#include <iostream>

int main() {
    std::cout << "Hello, C++ World!" << std::endl;
    return 0;
}
```

---

## 📊 Under the Hood

- **Memory**: How it affects the Stack and Heap.
- **Performance**: Zero-overhead principle (you don't pay for what you don't use).

---

## 🏁 Summary
Mastering Introduction to C++ brings you closer to being a Systems Architect. Keep coding!

backupQuiz.text: What is the primary purpose of Introduction to C++?
backupQuiz.explanation: C++ is primarily a systems programming language offering fine-grained control.

backupQuiz.text: In C++, Introduction to C++ is resolved at:
backupQuiz.explanation: Ideally compile time for templates/overloading, runtime for virtual functions.

backupQuiz.text: C++ manages memory...
backupQuiz.explanation: Traditionally manual (new/delete), but modern C++ uses RAII smart pointers.

backupQuiz.text: The main optimization goal of C++ is:
backupQuiz.explanation: You shouldn't accept overhead for features you don't use.

backupQuiz.text: Ready for more?
backupQuiz.explanation: Let's compile and run!

# 🚀 2. Installing Compiler & First Program

> **C++ Mastery:** Understand the difference between compilation errors and linker errors.

---

## 📖 Theoretical Foundation

To run C++, you need a compiler like GCC (g++), Clang, or MSVC. The compilation process involves Preprocessing, Compilation, Assembly, and Linking to produce an executable.

### ⚙️ Mechanics
- **Efficiency**: C++ gives you direct control over hardware.
- **Flexibility**: Use high-level abstractions or low-level pointers.
- **Safety**: Modern C++ (11/14/17/20) provides safer idioms.

---

## 💻 Code Example

```cpp
// command line
// g++ main.cpp -o app
// ./app
```

---

## 📊 Under the Hood

- **Memory**: How it affects the Stack and Heap.
- **Performance**: Zero-overhead principle (you don't pay for what you don't use).

---

## 🏁 Summary
Mastering Installing Compiler & First Program brings you closer to being a Systems Architect. Keep coding!

# 🚀 3. Tokens, Keywords & Identifiers

> **C++ Mastery:** Use snake_case or camelCase consistently.

---

## 📖 Theoretical Foundation

Tokens are the smallest building blocks. Keywords (`int`, `return`, `class`) are reserved. Identifiers are names for variables/functions. C++ is case-sensitive.

### ⚙️ Mechanics
- **Efficiency**: C++ gives you direct control over hardware.
- **Flexibility**: Use high-level abstractions or low-level pointers.
- **Safety**: Modern C++ (11/14/17/20) provides safer idioms.

---

## 💻 Code Example

```cpp
int myVariable = 10; // Valid
// int class = 5; // Error: keyword
```

---

## 📊 Under the Hood

- **Memory**: How it affects the Stack and Heap.
- **Performance**: Zero-overhead principle (you don't pay for what you don't use).

---

## 🏁 Summary
Mastering Tokens, Keywords & Identifiers brings you closer to being a Systems Architect. Keep coding!

# 🚀 4. Variables & Data Types

> **C++ Mastery:** Size of types depends on the architecture (32-bit vs 64-bit).

---

## 📖 Theoretical Foundation

C++ requires explicit types. Primitives: `int`, `float`, `double`, `char`, `bool`, `void`. Modifiers: `short`, `long`, `unsigned`. `auto` (C++11) allows type inference.

### ⚙️ Mechanics
- **Efficiency**: C++ gives you direct control over hardware.
- **Flexibility**: Use high-level abstractions or low-level pointers.
- **Safety**: Modern C++ (11/14/17/20) provides safer idioms.

---

## 💻 Code Example

```cpp
int age = 25;
double pi = 3.14159;
auto name = "Glinto"; // const char*
```

---

## 📊 Under the Hood

- **Memory**: How it affects the Stack and Heap.
- **Performance**: Zero-overhead principle (you don't pay for what you don't use).

---

## 🏁 Summary
Mastering Variables & Data Types brings you closer to being a Systems Architect. Keep coding!

lessons.backupContent:
# 🚀 5. Input & Output (cin, cout)

> **C++ Mastery:** `std::endl` flushes the buffer; `\n` is faster.

---

## 📖 Theoretical Foundation

The `<iostream>` library provides streams. `std::cout` prints to standard output. `std::cin` reads from standard input. `<<` is the insertion operator, `>>` is extraction.

### ⚙️ Mechanics
- **Efficiency**: C++ gives you direct control over hardware.
- **Flexibility**: Use high-level abstractions or low-level pointers.
- **Safety**: Modern C++ (11/14/17/20) provides safer idioms.

---

## 💻 Code Example

```cpp
int x;
std::cin >> x;
std::cout << "You entered: " << x;
```

---

## 📊 Under the Hood

- **Memory**: How it affects the Stack and Heap.
- **Performance**: Zero-overhead principle (you don't pay for what you don't use).

---

## 🏁 Summary
Mastering Input & Output (cin, cout) brings you closer to being a Systems Architect. Keep coding!

# 🚀 6. Operators in C++

> **C++ Mastery:** Bitwise operators (`&`, `|`, `^`, `<<`) are crucial for system optimization.

---

## 📖 Theoretical Foundation

Arithmetic, Relational, Logical, Bitwise, Assignment. The Ternary Operator `? :` is a concise if-else. Operator precedence determines evaluation order.

### ⚙️ Mechanics
- **Efficiency**: C++ gives you direct control over hardware.
- **Flexibility**: Use high-level abstractions or low-level pointers.
- **Safety**: Modern C++ (11/14/17/20) provides safer idioms.

---

## 💻 Code Example

```cpp
int a = 10, b = 20;
int max = (a > b) ? a : b;
```

---

## 📊 Under the Hood

- **Memory**: How it affects the Stack and Heap.
- **Performance**: Zero-overhead principle (you don't pay for what you don't use).

---

## 🏁 Summary
Mastering Operators in C++ brings you closer to being a Systems Architect. Keep coding!

lessons.backupContent:
# 🚀 7. Type Casting

> **C++ Mastery:** Avoid C-style casts; use static_cast, dynamic_cast, const_cast, reinterpret_cast.

---

## 📖 Theoretical Foundation

Implicit conversion happens automatically. Explicit casting (C-style `(int)x` or C++ style `static_cast<int>(x)`) forces conversion. `static_cast` is safer because it checks compile-time validity.

### ⚙️ Mechanics
- **Efficiency**: C++ gives you direct control over hardware.
- **Flexibility**: Use high-level abstractions or low-level pointers.
- **Safety**: Modern C++ (11/14/17/20) provides safer idioms.

---

## 💻 Code Example

```cpp
double d = 9.99;
int n = static_cast<int>(d); // 9 (truncated)
```

---

## 📊 Under the Hood

- **Memory**: How it affects the Stack and Heap.
- **Performance**: Zero-overhead principle (you don't pay for what you don't use).

---

## 🏁 Summary
Mastering Type Casting brings you closer to being a Systems Architect. Keep coding!

# 🚀 8. Constants & #define

> **C++ Mastery:** `constexpr` ensures values are computed at compile time.

---

## 📖 Theoretical Foundation

`const` variables cannot be changed. `#define` creates preprocessor macros (text replacement). `const` is preferred as it respects scope and type safety.

### ⚙️ Mechanics
- **Efficiency**: C++ gives you direct control over hardware.
- **Flexibility**: Use high-level abstractions or low-level pointers.
- **Safety**: Modern C++ (11/14/17/20) provides safer idioms.

---

## 💻 Code Example

```cpp
#define PI 3.14 // Macro
const double Pi = 3.14159; // Type-safe
```

---

## 📊 Under the Hood

- **Memory**: How it affects the Stack and Heap.
- **Performance**: Zero-overhead principle (you don't pay for what you don't use).

---

## 🏁 Summary
Mastering Constants & #define brings you closer to being a Systems Architect. Keep coding!

lessons.title: 9. if, else if, else
lessons.content:
### 🚧 Content Under Maintenance

This lesson is being updated soon!
lessons.backupContent:
# 🚀 9. if, else if, else

> **C++ Mastery:** Variables declared inside `if` condition are scoped to the block (C++17).

---

## 📖 Theoretical Foundation

Standard conditional branching. Scopes are defined by `{}`. Single-line statements don't strictly require braces but are recommended for safety.

### ⚙️ Mechanics
- **Efficiency**: C++ gives you direct control over hardware.
- **Flexibility**: Use high-level abstractions or low-level pointers.
- **Safety**: Modern C++ (11/14/17/20) provides safer idioms.

---

## 💻 Code Example

```cpp
if (score > 90) std::cout << "A";
else if (score > 80) std::cout << "B";
else std::cout << "C";
```

---

## 📊 Under the Hood

- **Memory**: How it affects the Stack and Heap.
- **Performance**: Zero-overhead principle (you don't pay for what you don't use).

---

## 🏁 Summary
Mastering if, else if, else brings you closer to being a Systems Architect. Keep coding!

# 🚀 10. switch Statement

> **C++ Mastery:** Fallthrough can be intentional but should be commented.

---

## 📖 Theoretical Foundation

Optimized dispatch for integral types (int, char, enum). Does not work with strings or floats. Use `break` to prevent fallthrough.

### ⚙️ Mechanics
- **Efficiency**: C++ gives you direct control over hardware.
- **Flexibility**: Use high-level abstractions or low-level pointers.
- **Safety**: Modern C++ (11/14/17/20) provides safer idioms.

---

## 💻 Code Example

```cpp
switch(grade) {
    case 'A': std::cout << "Great"; break;
    default: std::cout << "Pass";
}
```

---

## 📊 Under the Hood

- **Memory**: How it affects the Stack and Heap.
- **Performance**: Zero-overhead principle (you don't pay for what you don't use).

---

## 🏁 Summary
Mastering switch Statement brings you closer to being a Systems Architect. Keep coding!

# 🚀 11. for Loop

> **C++ Mastery:** Use `const auto&` in range loops to avoid copying complex objects.

---

## 📖 Theoretical Foundation

Traditional `for(init; condition; inc)`. Range-based for loop (C++11) `for(auto x : collection)` simplifies iteration over arrays/vectors.

### ⚙️ Mechanics
- **Efficiency**: C++ gives you direct control over hardware.
- **Flexibility**: Use high-level abstractions or low-level pointers.
- **Safety**: Modern C++ (11/14/17/20) provides safer idioms.

---

## 💻 Code Example

```cpp
int arr[] = {1, 2, 3};
for(int x : arr) std::cout << x << " ";
```

---

## 📊 Under the Hood

- **Memory**: How it affects the Stack and Heap.
- **Performance**: Zero-overhead principle (you don't pay for what you don't use).

---

## 🏁 Summary
Mastering for Loop brings you closer to being a Systems Architect. Keep coding!

# 🚀 12. while Loop

> **C++ Mastery:** Watch out for infinite loops in network listeners.

---

## 📖 Theoretical Foundation

Pre-condition loop. Runs 0 or more times.

### ⚙️ Mechanics
- **Efficiency**: C++ gives you direct control over hardware.
- **Flexibility**: Use high-level abstractions or low-level pointers.
- **Safety**: Modern C++ (11/14/17/20) provides safer idioms.

---

## 💻 Code Example

```cpp
while(running) { process(); }
```

---

## 📊 Under the Hood

- **Memory**: How it affects the Stack and Heap.
- **Performance**: Zero-overhead principle (you don't pay for what you don't use).

---

## 🏁 Summary
Mastering while Loop brings you closer to being a Systems Architect. Keep coding!

# 🚀 13. do-while Loop

> **C++ Mastery:** Useful for menu-driven programs.

---

## 📖 Theoretical Foundation

Post-condition loop. Runs 1 or more times.

### ⚙️ Mechanics
- **Efficiency**: C++ gives you direct control over hardware.
- **Flexibility**: Use high-level abstractions or low-level pointers.
- **Safety**: Modern C++ (11/14/17/20) provides safer idioms.

---

## 💻 Code Example

```cpp
do {
    std::cin >> input;
} while(input != 'q');
```

---

## 📊 Under the Hood

- **Memory**: How it affects the Stack and Heap.
- **Performance**: Zero-overhead principle (you don't pay for what you don't use).

---

## 🏁 Summary
Mastering do-while Loop brings you closer to being a Systems Architect. Keep coding!

lessons.title: 14. break & continue
lessons.content:
### 🚧 Content Under Maintenance

lessons.backupContent:
# 🚀 14. break & continue

> **C++ Mastery:** Overusing simple control flow tools can create 'spaghetti code'.

---

## 📖 Theoretical Foundation

`break` exits the loop immediately. `continue` skips the rest of the body and jumps to the next iteration check.

### ⚙️ Mechanics
- **Efficiency**: C++ gives you direct control over hardware.
- **Flexibility**: Use high-level abstractions or low-level pointers.
- **Safety**: Modern C++ (11/14/17/20) provides safer idioms.

---

## 💻 Code Example

```cpp
for(int i=0; i<10; i++) {
    if(i%2==0) continue;
    std::cout << i;
}
```

---

## 📊 Under the Hood

- **Memory**: How it affects the Stack and Heap.
- **Performance**: Zero-overhead principle (you don't pay for what you don't use).

---

## 🏁 Summary
Mastering break & continue brings you closer to being a Systems Architect. Keep coding!

lessons.backupContent:
# 🚀 15. Functions in C++

> **C++ Mastery:** Function signatures include name and parameter types.

---

## 📖 Theoretical Foundation

Blocks of code performing a task. Must be declared (prototype) before use if defined after `main`. Return type `void` means no return value.

### ⚙️ Mechanics
- **Efficiency**: C++ gives you direct control over hardware.
- **Flexibility**: Use high-level abstractions or low-level pointers.
- **Safety**: Modern C++ (11/14/17/20) provides safer idioms.

---

## 💻 Code Example

```cpp
int add(int a, int b);
 // Prototype
int main() { add(1, 2); }
int add(int a, int b) { return a + b; }
```

---

## 📊 Under the Hood

- **Memory**: How it affects the Stack and Heap.
- **Performance**: Zero-overhead principle (you don't pay for what you don't use).

---

## 🏁 Summary
Mastering Functions in C++ brings you closer to being a Systems Architect. Keep coding!

# 🚀 16. Function Arguments

> **C++ Mastery:** Do not redefine default args in both declaration and definition.

---

## 📖 Theoretical Foundation

Parameters are local automatic variables. Default arguments `void func(int x=10)` exist but must be trailing paremeters.

### ⚙️ Mechanics
- **Efficiency**: C++ gives you direct control over hardware.
- **Flexibility**: Use high-level abstractions or low-level pointers.
- **Safety**: Modern C++ (11/14/17/20) provides safer idioms.

---

## 💻 Code Example

```cpp
void log(std::string msg, int level=1);
```

---

## 📊 Under the Hood

- **Memory**: How it affects the Stack and Heap.
- **Performance**: Zero-overhead principle (you don't pay for what you don't use).

---

## 🏁 Summary
Mastering Function Arguments brings you closer to being a Systems Architect. Keep coding!

lessons.title: 17. Call by Value & Call by Reference
lessons.content:
### 🚧 Content Under Maintenance

This lesson is being updated soon!
lessons.backupContent:
# 🚀 17. Call by Value & Call by Reference

> **C++ Mastery:** Use `const Type&` to pass large objects efficiently without modification.

---

## 📖 Theoretical Foundation

Call by Value copies data (expensive for objects). Call by Reference `&` passes the memory address, allowing modification and avoiding copy.

### ⚙️ Mechanics
- **Efficiency**: C++ gives you direct control over hardware.
- **Flexibility**: Use high-level abstractions or low-level pointers.
- **Safety**: Modern C++ (11/14/17/20) provides safer idioms.

---

## 💻 Code Example

```cpp
void swap(int &a, int &b) {
    int temp = a; a = b; b = temp;
}
```

---

## 📊 Under the Hood

- **Memory**: How it affects the Stack and Heap.
- **Performance**: Zero-overhead principle (you don't pay for what you don't use).

---

## 🏁 Summary
Mastering Call by Value & Call by Reference brings you closer to being a Systems Architect. Keep coding!

backupQuiz.text: What is the primary purpose of Call by Value & Call by Reference?
backupQuiz.explanation: C++ is primarily a systems programming language offering fine-grained control.

backupQuiz.text: In C++, Call by Value & Call by Reference is resolved at:
backupQuiz.explanation: Ideally compile time for templates/overloading, runtime for virtual functions.

backupQuiz.text: C++ manages memory...
backupQuiz.explanation: Traditionally manual (new/delete), but modern C++ uses RAII smart pointers.

backupQuiz.text: The main optimization goal of C++ is:
backupQuiz.explanation: You shouldn't accept overhead for features you don't use.

backupQuiz.text: Ready for more?

lessons.title: 18. Inline Functions
lessons.content:
### 🚧 Content Under Maintenance

This lesson is being updated soon!
lessons.backupContent:
# 🚀 18. Inline Functions

> **C++ Mastery:** Modern compilers often ignore `inline` hint and decide for themselves.

---

## 📖 Theoretical Foundation

`inline` suggests the compiler replace the call with the function body code to eliminate call overhead. Good for small getters/setters.

### ⚙️ Mechanics
- **Efficiency**: C++ gives you direct control over hardware.
- **Flexibility**: Use high-level abstractions or low-level pointers.
- **Safety**: Modern C++ (11/14/17/20) provides safer idioms.

---

## 💻 Code Example

```cpp
inline int square(int x) { return x*x; }
```

---

## 📊 Under the Hood

- **Memory**: How it affects the Stack and Heap.
- **Performance**: Zero-overhead principle (you don't pay for what you don't use).

---

## 🏁 Summary
Mastering Inline Functions brings you closer to being a Systems Architect. Keep coding!

lessons.title: 19. Recursion
lessons.content:
### 🚧 Content Under Maintenance

This lesson is being updated soon!
lessons.backupContent:
# 🚀 19. Recursion

> **C++ Mastery:** Deep recursion can cause Stack Overflow.

---

## 📖 Theoretical Foundation

A function calling itself. Requires a Base Case to stop. Uses the call stack.

### ⚙️ Mechanics
- **Efficiency**: C++ gives you direct control over hardware.
- **Flexibility**: Use high-level abstractions or low-level pointers.
- **Safety**: Modern C++ (11/14/17/20) provides safer idioms.

---

## 💻 Code Example

```cpp
int fact(int n) {
    if(n<=1) return 1;
    return n * fact(n-1);
}
```

---

## 📊 Under the Hood

- **Memory**: How it affects the Stack and Heap.
- **Performance**: Zero-overhead principle (you don't pay for what you don't use).

---

## 🏁 Summary
Mastering Recursion brings you closer to being a Systems Architect. Keep coding!

lessons.title: 20. Storage Classes
lessons.content:
### 🚧 Content Under Maintenance

This lesson is being updated soon!
lessons.backupContent:
# 🚀 20. Storage Classes

> **C++ Mastery:** Static local variables initialize only once.

---

## 📖 Theoretical Foundation

`auto` (default), `static` (persists for program life), `extern` (global visibility), `mutable` (modifiable in const objects).

### ⚙️ Mechanics
- **Efficiency**: C++ gives you direct control over hardware.
- **Flexibility**: Use high-level abstractions or low-level pointers.
- **Safety**: Modern C++ (11/14/17/20) provides safer idioms.

---

## 💻 Code Example

```cpp
void count() {
    static int c = 0;
    c++;
    std::cout << c;
}
```

---

## 📊 Under the Hood

- **Memory**: How it affects the Stack and Heap.
- **Performance**: Zero-overhead principle (you don't pay for what you don't use).

---

## 🏁 Summary
Mastering Storage Classes brings you closer to being a Systems Architect. Keep coding!
